entries.title: Facts and Figures 2025: Internet use
entries.claim: 74 per cent of the world's population were online in 2025.

entries.title: Facts and Figures 2025: Internet use in urban and rural areas
entries.claim: 58 per cent of rural dwellers used the internet, compared with 85 per cent of urban dwellers.

entries.title: Facts and Figures 2025: Mobile phone ownership
entries.claim: 82 per cent of individuals aged 10 years or older owned a mobile phone.

entries.title: Facts and Figures 2025
entries.claim: More than 96 per cent of the global population was covered by a mobile broadband network.
entries.caveat: Coverage does not prove use, affordability, suitable hardware or skills.

entries.title: Facts and Figures 2025: Affordability of ICT services
entries.claim: 130 of 205 economies met the mobile-broadband affordability target.
entries.caveat: Economies are not weighted by population.

entries.title: Languages matter: global guidance on multilingual education
entries.claim: UNESCO reports that 40 per cent lack access to education in a language they speak and understand fluently.
entries.caveat: The estimate is widely reused and was refreshed in 2025 guidance, but its underlying global observation period is not presented as a single current year.

entries.title: Monitoring education in the SDGs
entries.claim: The global out-of-school rate was around 17 per cent in 2024, representing 273 million children and youth.

entries.title: Monitoring education in the SDGs
entries.claim: 88 per cent completed primary education on time in 2024.

entries.title: Monitoring education in the SDGs
entries.claim: 58 per cent of students achieve minimum reading proficiency at the end of primary.
entries.caveat: The estimate has not been updated since COVID-19.

entries.title: Monitoring education in the SDGs
entries.claim: 44 per cent of students achieve minimum mathematics proficiency at the end of primary.
entries.caveat: The estimate has not been updated since COVID-19.

entries.organisation: Tracking SDG 7 custodian agencies / World Bank
entries.title: Tracking SDG 7: The Energy Progress Report 2026
entries.claim: Global electricity access remained at 92 per cent in 2024; 655 million people lacked access.

entries.organisation: Tracking SDG 7 custodian agencies / World Bank
entries.title: Tracking SDG 7: The Energy Progress Report 2026
entries.claim: Approximately one quarter of the world's population, around two billion people, lacked clean cooking access.
entries.caveat: The dashboard displays 75 as an approximate inverse of the reported one-quarter gap.

entries.title: World Social Protection Report 2024–26, Chapter 4
entries.claim: 23.9 per cent of children aged 0 to 18 were covered by child cash benefits; 1.8 billion were not covered.

entries.title: The State of the World's Children 2025
entries.claim: 412 million children lived in extreme monetary poverty on less than US$3 per day.
entries.caveat: Not included in the score because this source presentation does not supply the matching global child denominator alongside the claim.

entries.title: Giga – Connecting Every School to the Internet
entries.claim: Only around half of the world's schools are online; more than one billion children lack quality fixed broadband at home.
entries.caveat: Used only as a proxy for institutional bridging.

providerAccessGate.claim: ChatGPT is not meant for children under 13; an adult must conduct interactions in educational contexts for under-13s.
Claude.ai is not offered to users under 18.
Copilot is generally available to users over 18 and has expanded access to ages 13–18 subject to regional minimum ages.
Parents can enable supervised under-13 access in supported regions, but it is unavailable for supervised accounts in the UK, EEA and Switzerland.

frameworkEvidence.title: Guidance on AI and children, version 3.0
frameworkEvidence.claim: UNICEF defines ten requirements for child-centred AI.
frameworkEvidence.usage: Framework for the future safety-and-rights dimension, not an implementation score.

frameworkEvidence.title: AI for children
frameworkEvidence.claim: Children remain largely excluded from shaping AI systems, especially in low-resource settings, and evidence on developmental effects is lacking.
frameworkEvidence.usage: Evidence that agency and participation remain unmeasured critical dimensions.
